title: Audio Transcription Flow
generate_flow: PendingTranscriptionGeneration → Ai::fakeableTranscriptionProvider(provider)  [resolves real or fake provider via AiManager]
PendingTranscriptionGeneration → TranscriptionProvider::transcribe(audio, language, diarize, model, timeout)
On FailoverableException: fire ProviderFailedOver event, continue to next provider in list
If all providers fail: throw last FailoverableException
queue_flow: Guard: audio MUST be StoredAudio or LocalAudio (Base64Audio not allowed for queue)
GenerateTranscription::handle() → pendingTranscription::generate(provider, model)  [same flow as synchronous]
key_relationships: AiManager extends MultipleInstanceManager; uses InteractsWithFakeTranscriptions trait
TranscriptionProvider (interface) defines transcribe(), transcriptionGateway(), useTranscriptionGateway(), defaultTranscriptionModel()
GenerateTranscription (Job) holds PendingTranscriptionGeneration and calls generate() async
PendingTranscriptionGeneration uses Conditionable trait (when/unless fluent helpers)
response_types: QueuedTranscriptionResponse — wraps PendingDispatch (or FakePendingDispatch); returned by queue()
failover_mechanism: Provider list supports multiple fallback providers; FailoverableException triggers ProviderFailedOver event and iteration to next provider
fake_support: Ai::fakeableTranscriptionProvider() clones real provider and swaps in FakeTranscriptionGateway; queue() path records QueuedTranscriptionPrompt instead of dispatching job
important_notes: timeout param is passed directly to provider::transcribe(); queue() enforces StoredAudio/LocalAudio only (Base64 not serialisable); provider resolved from config 'ai.default_for_transcription' if not specified
